title: coincya johnstonii
intro: Coincya johnstonii, commonly known as Cornish kale, is a species of plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive function. The therapeutic actions of Coincya johnstonii include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-diabetic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Coincya johnstonii can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues. Improper use of this plant has been linked to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in some individuals. In rare cases, excessive consumption of Coincya johnstonii can also cause dizziness, headaches, and respiratory problems. It is essential to consult a medical professional or a qualified herbalist before using Coincya johnstonii for medicinal purposes.
